summary: Fail: the Twilio restart coverage is solid, but the Cloudflare transport still omits the JS realtime SDK marker header, so the fetch-upgrade request does not yet preserve the full required header contract for VAL-EXT-001.

issues.description: Add the missing `X-OpenAI-Agents-SDK` header to Cloudflare upgrade requests. `build_upgrade_request` only emits Authorization, Connection, Sec-WebSocket-Protocol, Upgrade, and User-Agent, but the JS source of truth forwards the full realtime common-header bundle and includes `X-OpenAI-Agents-SDK`. This means the Rust Cloudflare transport still falls short of the required-header parity promised by the feature and VAL-EXT-001.
issues.evidence: crates/agents-extensions/src/extensions/realtime_transports.rs:58-68 only sets Authorization/Connection/Sec-WebSocket-Protocol/Upgrade/User-Agent; reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-extensions/src/CloudflareRealtimeTransport.ts:57-61 forwards `...this.getCommonRequestHeaders()`; reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-realtime/src/utils.ts:315-318 defines that common header set with `X-OpenAI-Agents-SDK`; crates/openai-agents/tests/extensions_semantics.rs:25-29 only asserts Authorization, so the omission is not covered by the new facade test.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state says Cloudflare must send the 'required headers' but never enumerates the exact realtime header bundle, which left room to miss the JS `X-OpenAI-Agents-SDK` header while still appearing compliant.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Add an explicit note in the parity/library guidance that Cloudflare realtime transport parity includes the JS common realtime headers (`User-Agent` and `X-OpenAI-Agents-SDK`) so future workers and reviewers can validate the contract precisely.

commands.observation: Confirmed the reviewed commit and that it introduced the extension realtime transport implementation plus facade tests for this feature.
Reviewed the implementation and test diff; the Cloudflare request builder omits `X-OpenAI-Agents-SDK`, and the new facade test does not assert that header.
Verified the reviewed commit id resolves in the repository.
